item.description: Never forget. Source : https://twitter.com/LakotaMan1/status/1520760312995016704
item.dc:creator: Pentjotal

item.title: Glad that's cleared up.
item.description:
Long personal post warning - Unfortunately, after I lost custody of all my friends in my divorce, and my brother already put up with me "swooning over girls in our teens," I have no one else to tell my story. Feel free to ignore if you don't like reading. Previously on Imgur: https://imgur.com/gallery/i7gQTjZ In short, went on date with a woman. Despite what I understood as physical interest, she hardly said anything. Confused me came to Imgur to vent, the results of which can be seen above. I sent a message to the mutual friend who set her and I up on the date and asked "Why exactly did you think that would be a good idea?" It turns out there were a couple of problems. The first Imgur spotted very quickly. To quote @RingoAteMyBaby (among a number of others): "As someone on the spectrum, she might be on the spectrum." Apparently she is indeed someone with ASD. My date had called our mutual friend to ask if the friend had told me about the ASD, because apparently I was very understanding without being told anything. Score one for accidental victories? The second problem was a language issue, in that she's not a native speaker of my language, having moved here a few years ago. As she has a name that could easily be from somewhere nearby, I made an assumption. I was told she prefers to communicate in English, but didn't know if she could "change the parameters" of our date (or something. Best translation of what she told our mutual friend.) This is not something I was able to notice on the date myself, as she spoke so little I couldn't have noticed. So I took more of Imgur's advice and asked my mutual friend what my date thought about me and received an annoyingly uninformative "You should contact her." So...
I sent a message following advice from the original thread, very directly saying "Our previous date confused me. I am interested in you. I'd like to take you out again. And as you can see, I speak English. Are you interested? One word answer is perfectly valid." And I asked for it... in ten seconds I received the response "Yes." That was it. Followed two minutes later by "Today?" Short notice. But fine. Then a third message arrived. "I struggle to process surprise. Can you tell me your plan?" Well, I knew a couple of things about her from our mutual friend at this point. She loves to read, and she's obsessed with tea. I built my date around that and suggested going to the library's quiet reading section, read some books, maybe discuss them and drink some tea. The responses I received were... enthusiastic to the say the least. Including several paragraphs about books she likes, along with shelf references so I can find them in the library. She also mentioned...
This is unfortunately something I've heard before. When our mutual friend described me, she said I was a "nice, quiet nerd who spends his time working with computers." She neglected to mention that I'm a 6'5 metalhead that looks like a viking. And not one of the hot, made for TV vikings. Think dad-bod Thor with more beard than face and tattoos everywhere but my dick. It takes people a moment to adjust to the fact I'm a gentle giant. It clearly took her three hours. Regardless, I head to the mainland and meet up with her outside her apartment. We start walking towards the library and she immediately takes my hand, which is a positive. She also doesn't say a word, but I'm prepared for that by now. Once we're at the library, she does start talking. She even asks me a few questions. But they're all utterly random. Like "how long have you been growing your beard?" and "how do you feel about men wearing nail polish?" and other non sequiturs. She also opens up about herself a bit more. Tells me that she can only read books where the language is unsophisticated. IE: She can read Dan Brown, but couldn't manage Stephen King. Which I think I can understand. Direct language is important? And if Stephen King wrote about a person eating an apple, it would be 9 pages long. And written from the viewpoint of the apple itself. And then...
She pulls out her phone, first time I've seen the thing. She sends me a text message asking if I want to eat something. When we're one meter away from each other. Literally sat at the same table. I guess she's more comfortable with text-based communication. Which is fine. We leave the library, go to eat. Not much happens there, except she becomes a little more talkative. Subjectively, at least. She's still not what someone would call chatty, but it's also very clear now that she's showing interest hapticly (haptically?) rather than vocally. But it was also the site of the rather comical conversation: Me: "Do you like music?" Her: "You'll have to be a lot more specific." Me: "Do you like live music?" Her: "That's only a little more specific." At which point I told her about an open mic night I used to frequent before Covid that re-opened. People play covers for the most part, so familiar music. She asks about the volume and, in the longest speech for her so far, explains about something called sensory overload. In the end, we figured out that it would probably be okay, and went to listen to the music. Unfortunately there were some familiar faces at the bar who pushed me into performing. I was unsure about leaving my date alone, but she actually encouraged me. So... what is there to lose, right?
This was her comment. Did I just get... autism-negged?! Honestly, this was the funniest thing that happened the whole evening. After this, the story becomes rather normal. There was a walk home, there was a kiss, etc. By the time I was back on my boat and heading home, though, she'd already sent me a text message saying that she'd enjoyed the evening and would like to see me again... accompanied by a detailed schedule of her availability for the next two weeks. So thanks, boys and girls. You got me at least one date, and looks like another on the horizon. The moral of this story: Listen to Imgur's advice. It seems we collectively know our shit.
item.dc:creator: 0xEF

item.title: A living hell for 4 years. My journey through Glossopharyngeal neuralgia *graphic warning* (contains internal anatomy photos)
item.description:
4 years ago I had awaken to the worst pain I could fathom and very hard to accurately describe. The right side of my throat physically felt on fire and also like I had swallowed a handful of hornets. Symptoms changed from day to day. Sometimes my right tonsil felt ready to pop from painful pressure, other times I would have horrible migraine attacks accompanied with vomiting, then the worst sensation of being stabbed in the neck. The fatigue was crippling, leaving me fixated to the bed/couch/floor whenever I had the option. Every doctor I went to could not figure it out (and I believe some assumed I was fabricating the ordeal). After seeing 3 ENTs, 1 GI, 1 oral pain specialist, and even a neurologist I felt as though I had been given up on. There were little choices left other than return to my ENT and opt for a Hail Mary, a request to remove my tonsils. The ENT was adamant that it probably would not help but performed the procedure anyway. Word of advise: if you’re an adult and it’s not necessary, don’t have them removed. The pain is horrible and not worth it in the end.
It helped for a couple weeks and I have no idea why, but the pain returned just as I stepped on a plane bound for the desert.
6 months and nothing more than a tent for a clinic left me little choice but to wait until I returned home. I held tight and hit the ground running as soon my boots hit soil again. But there was nothing to show for progress. 6 months of being back home and I still had no idea what was wrong with me. I continued reaching out to doctor after doctor and was always sent away feeling more hopeless and alone. Some doctors even personally suggested that I seek mental health and this could all be fabricated in my mind.
(My best boy Indie) I was at the breaking point. Most of my time in a clinic evolved from seeking diagnosis to proving different MDs theories wrong in attempt to be referred to different specialist offices. One notion of acid reflux+12 weeks of taking meds+having my stomach video scoped to prove I don’t have acid reflux=1 ENT referral. Took antidepressants for 3 months to prove I’m not a mental case. Ya’ll get the point…. All the while my symptoms were increasing and changing.
At last I met the person who believed me! I was asked to sit down with an oral pain specialist one day. Her first words were that she wasn’t an oral pain specialist, just a dentist. There seemed to be some sort of mixup as to why I was sent to her office. None the less, she was going to find out what was wrong with me since I had been given up on. For four days I was interviewed about the previous 3 years of pain. When she was finished, I was given my diagnosis…Glossopharyngeal neuralgia. I cried as she read aloud the symptoms accompanied with the condition.
One simple MRI later, and there it was. My artery was contacting and stimulating my cranial nerve. The first neurosurgeon explained that it was a very rare condition (especially in someone as young as myself) and there was a brain surgery to correct it. However, he could not perform the procedure, nor could the next 4 surgeons we went to. The biggest problem was the lack of experience. One day a phone call came in from my case manager. She explained that they had found a very experienced surgeon in the university of Ohio, turns out he was one of the best in the world. A little red tape and a few weeks put me on a 16 hr flight to Ohio. As I laid on the operating table, I couldn’t help but feel excited yet terrified simultaneously. Either way, my consciousness faded as I waved at the surgeon prepping.
Welcome to the inside of my noggin. The pink tube is my artery routing incorrectly and contacting my nerves. One intern explained to myself later that the room gasp when this image appeared on the fiberscope camera. The procedure was scheduled to last 4 hours but had to extend to 7 when they opened me up and found a bigger problem. As seen above, my artery was contacting 4 of the nerves, not 1. This made all of my symptoms finally make sense.
This is the fix, and what I like to refer to as proper cable management. The white substance is a Teflon sponge permanently affixed inside the cavity to prevent migration.
Here is the incision post op and I can say that it is now barely noticeable to others. When I had awaken, I cried. The pain was completely absent and it was the first time in four years that everything felt normal. No longer did I ponder how this would somehow kill me, gone would be the days of being immobilized, and finally I could live life with my family again.
If you ever have to live with this condition, please know that you are not alone. Contact me anytime and ask any question. To the dentist that believed my story when it seemed no one else did, I’m naming my next child after you. I would not have mentally survived without your interjection. To the surgeon that corrected me, I can never repay the life which you have returned to normal. Expect modest gifts whenever I can send them.

item.title: Interspecies trading with birds
item.description: "What do you do for a living?" "Oh I trade nuts for money with crows" Trading has been documented from every known human society and it's been done for a very long time. Seems even some birds can do it, crows are some of the most intelligent species on the planet. Now who would teach crows some socially sustainable distribution of nuts?

item.title: Down from 300lbs to 173lbs in 18 months *WEIGHT LOSS PROGRESSION PHOTO UPDATE*
item.description:
Boneless center cut pork chop seasoned with Penzey's Bavarian herb blend and pink salt, seared in ghee Sauteed shallots, garlic and white mushrooms in ghee 5oz gently wilted spinach Blueberries Nightly apple cider vinegar cocktail. 1tbsp ACV diluted in 2oz pedialyte and 12oz water Workout notes: Some pilates style stretching to warm the core and treadmill for 20minutes. I walked 2 dogs for 30 minutes each up hills in the woods at the shelter; Maggie and Tater Tot. Also, I cleaned 4 kennels for a total of 100 minutes volunteering. It's worth documenting that I'm feeling pooped going to the shelter after a good workout. Even though I eat recovery foods between the gym and the shelter, I have noticed that since quitting dairy 2 months ago that my energy levels are a bit unpredictable. I used to have an ounce or two of cheese in my recovery snacks and like 3 Chobani a day. Turns out dairy is very bad for my skin disease, Hidradenitis. I'm making all the moves to eventually get surgeries I desperately need. However, I have to raise a ton cash for the expenses not covered by insurance. I ask anyone who is inspired by my story to share my GoFundMe link in my imgur bio on your social media in hopes to get the medically necessary surgeries I need before I am a senior citizen! Insurance only covers so much https://imgur.com/user/tasteofnoise/about
As the economy tanks, grocery stores are reducing more and more perishable product. I got 1.18lbs of prime center cut pork chops for $2.62. Keep an eye out for reduced savings. Most grocery stores tend to markdown meat in the early morning while produce is often reduced in the evening. Ask the team members at your grocery store! I work in a grocery store so I go after SO MUCH reduced products.
Sound on for a little ASMR if this qualifies. Look at that crust!
I decided to go through my posts and see where I was last year. 13 months ago I made my first progression post. https://imgur.com/gallery/f1e1N7D
This is about a week ago. I have shaved that monstrosity off my face since! This is the creepiest look I could muster for entertainment value.
Sorry, not sorry for the selfie. A shot of my temporary bridge; soon to get the permanent in a couple more weeks. Resolving the missing and broken teeth from being beaten by my half brother when I was 18 was a huge step towards my mental health recovery. I had to look at that trauma in the mirror for nearly 20 years. I've had so much head trauma in my early life I'm amazed I can tie my shoes.
Secret Kitty Meeting Tax As I was leaving the shelter I interrupted this meeting. Black and white kitty was shooting me daggers
MAGGIE TAX! This is the current love of my life. Bitches come and go so often in my life now I'm having a hard time remembering all their names! Shit well, my friends!
item.dc:creator: TasteOfNoise

item.title: The Best News of Last Week — May 2, 2022
item.description:
1. Jacky Hunt-Broersma, a cancer survivor and amputee, sets world record by running 102 marathons in 102 days - She has documented all of her runs (and more) on her Instagram! It's @ncrunnerjacky
2. Black cat known for huge eyes, wonky feet to become mayor of Hell, Michigan. - Jinx the cat to become mayor of Hell, Michigan. This little black cat will rule over Hell, located about 20 miles northwest of Ann Arbor.
3. LeVar Burton, the beloved former Reading Rainbow host, will receive a Lifetime Achievement Award at the inaugural Children's and Family Emmys in December, the National Academy of Television Arts and Sciences announced this week.
4. Plastic-eating Enzyme Could Eliminate Billions of Tons of Landfill Waste - An enzyme variant created by engineers and scientists at The University of Texas at Austin can break down environment-throttling plastics that typically take centuries to degrade in just a matter of hours to days. This discovery was published yesterday in Nature
5. US Army replaces cake it stole from Italian girl in 1945 - Meri Mion - who turns 90 on Friday - was 13 when her village of San Pietro, near Vicenza, saw fighting between US troops and German soldiers. Americans. Sgt Peter Wallis, who handed over the dessert, said it was "a little awkward" to replace the stolen goods, "but it makes me feel great".
6. NAU to provide tuition-free college education for Arizona residents with household incomes at or below $65,000
7. EU reveals plans for the 'largest ever ban' on toxic chemicals - Thousands of potentially toxic or harmful chemicals still used in cosmetics, toys and even baby products could soon be banned in Europe under new regulations.
That's it for this week :) Until next week, you can read my previous posts and subscribe here https://feelgoodnews.substack.com/ for a weekly collection of inspirational, feel good news stories from around the globe.
item.dc:creator: FeelGoodNews
